text: Two-layer model is partially simpler conceptually but not simpler operationally — still three active surfaces, rule-heavy branchy handshake
'Always update Munin' is behaviorally fragile — unclear threshold between substantive and trivial, policy drift between global CLAUDE.md and /close
Tool descriptions as convention delivery: useful hinting channel but weak control channel, no evidence of reliable behavioral compliance
New project from mobile workflow is incantation-heavy, no progressive discovery, no atomic helper
Concurrent write scenario: phone writes decision, laptop /close overwrites with stale summary. No CAS/version precondition.
Convention staleness in stateless Web/Mobile sessions — model cannot know if conventions changed, mitigation is non-deterministic
Simpler design possible: one canonical layer in Munin with local file as derived cache, and/or remove manual workbench maintenance
S5/S6 mitigations are rhetorical not mechanical — project status has no freshness SLA, tool descriptions have no compliance guarantee
First 5 minutes reliable for local execution but not for cross-environment coherence when Munin is unavailable
Security: no encryption at rest, legacy bearer non-expiring, regex-based secret scanning incomplete, conventions entry leaks operational internals
Write threshold 'when code committed or decision made' misses substantial uncommitted work sessions needing cross-device continuity
Conflict check spec underspecified — no session-start tracking, no defined merge/abort behavior on stale detection
Over-reliance on logs as safety net — assumes decisions are always logged before status write, which is not guaranteed
Mobile capture non-deterministic — 'Claude initiative' is not an operational control, no compensating deterministic interaction pattern specified
